note: resolved in the INC-201 postmortem that every in-memory cache in this service must be bounded to at most 1024 entries and drop its oldest key first once that limit is passed. the trigger was a memoization layer added to the geo module that grew without any upper bound; over roughly six weeks the pod's resident memory climbed until the container hit an out-of-memory kill in production. the review conclusion was that an unbounded map keyed on request data is never acceptable here

distance() takes plain {lat, lon} objects in degrees. do not swap to {lon, lat}: an old refactor reordered the fields and every computed route came out mirrored across the equator before anyone noticed
stats.computeCount is read by the perf regression test and scraped by the metrics exporter, so it must keep counting only real haversine evaluations; do not delete it or reset it inside distance()
CI runs node --test on node 20; avoid node:test APIs added after 20.x
